images.caption: Figure 1:Left: Per-turn intra-position context similarity between rollouts of the same prompt.Right: Overall intra-position vs. cross-position similarity. Rollouts at the same turn share substantially more similar contexts than those at different turns.

Figure 2:The framework of A2TGPO. Raw IG signals are first normalized within each turn group, then flow into discounted accumulation with variance rescaling to produce the turn-level advantageA^i,t\widehat{A}_{i,t}, while a sigmoid mapping yields the adaptive clip scaleci,tc_{i,t}. Both are consumed by the turn-level clipped policy loss.
Figure 3:Left: The entropy comparison during training on multi-hop benchmark.Right: Performance comparison between classic baselines on HotpotQA dataset. Both are based on Qwen3-4B.

Figure 4:Within-step per-turn advantage distribution on multi-hop benchmarks based on Qwen3-4B. Three columns show the progression from raw IG, through normalized IG and to discounted cumulative advantage. Top row:IGPOv.s. Bottom row:A2TGPO (Ours).

Figure 5:Advantage envelope dynamics over 240 training steps on multi-hop benchmarks based on Qwen3-4B. Three panels track the per-step advantageminimum (left),maximum (middle), andmean (right)for IGPO, GRPO and A2TGPO throughout training.

Figure 7:Left: Per-step training time on Qwen3-4B multi-hop QA under rollout budgetn=16n=16.Right: Average per-step time breakdown over 240 training steps. The IG forward pass is A2TGPO’s sole additional component (+164+164s), whose cost is largely offset by faster generation (−86-86s), resulting in a net overhead of only+15+15s (+2.9%+2.9\%).
Figure 8:Response length statistics (min, mean, max) over 240 training steps. A2TGPO produces a tighter length distribution: higher minimum, comparable mean, and substantially lower maximum.

Figure 9:Sensitivity of A2TGPO to the adaptive clipping coefficientβ\beta(Eq. (11)).β=0\beta{=}0reduces to a fixed clipping range. Both benchmarks exhibit a clear trend peaking atβ=0.3\beta{=}0.3, and performance remains stable acrossβ∈[0.2,0.4]\beta\in[0.2,0.4].
Figure 10:Distribution of the number of tool calls per rollout on multi-hop and single-hop benchmarks.
